scope: actual LI sparse-MLA shared latent-KV backward, S4096 layer 2, BF16, 1xMI350
question: Can the compressed-group union operand keep the query-major producer and materialize the dKV-slice win into full sparse-attention backward latency?
answer: No for this implementation. The schedule is correct, but full source-boundary dQ+dKV is 28.78-29.02 ms versus the retained 11.13 ms sparse-attention backward gate.
read: The user's correction is right: union must remain an epilogue/output organization on top of the query-major source boundary, not a route/group-major owner for the whole kernel.
This implementation keeps token/query-major CTA placement, but it changes the selected operand axis from per-token selected slots to the compressed-group union for a token tile.
That union axis is not free for dQ. A four-token tile often has many groups that are valid for only a subset of tokens, so QK/PV/softmax/scaled-dP and dQ are replayed over masked union columns.
The dKV-only split still looks near the retained gate, but the actual full dQ+dKV boundary is the promotion metric and it loses badly.

The next viable design should keep the baseline selected-slot QK/PV/dQ path intact and attach group-aware dKV reduction after scaled-dP is available, instead of making union groups the operand axis for dQ.
next_kernel_contract: selected-slot query-major producer for QK/PV/softmax/scaled-dP/dQ, with a separate group-aware dKV epilogue that consumes the same scaled-dP page before HBM and reduces only the dKV write path by compressed group
